final_answer: 1. No, the sprinklers do not run this cycle (tank is at 15%, which is ≤20%).
2. Yes, the refill valve activates to fill the tank from the mains supply.

3. With the rain sensor removed, the timer activates the pump at 6 AM regardless of rain. Since the tank is at 15% (≤20%), the pump activates the refill valve and no water goes to the sprinklers. Same outcome as the current cycle: sprinklers off, refill valve on.
working: Q1: No rain detected, so timer activates pump at 6 AM. Pump checks tank: 15% which is ≤20%, so pump does NOT send water to sprinklers.
Q2: Because tank is ≤20%, the pump activates the refill valve instead. So yes, refill valve activates.

Q3: Without rain sensor, timer always fires at 6 AM. Pump activates regardless of rain. Tank at 15% ≤20%, so refill valve activates, sprinklers do not run. The rain condition becomes irrelevant once the sensor is removed.
